sourceMovie.title: Haywire
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Haywire for the same reason it broke through in 2011: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Steven Soderbergh keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Blood Diamond
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Haywire clicked for you, Blood Diamond (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Haywire clicked for you, Ice Road: Vengeance (2025) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Haywire?
faq.answer: Try Blood Diamond, Ice Road: Vengeance, The Substitute—each shares DNA with Haywire while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Haywire?
faq.answer: Haywire (2011) is best described as Action, Thriller, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Haywire worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
